mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) raises nominal revenue expectations for S&P 500 constituents and signals demand strength, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive CPI acceleration does not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 52% of months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.5% signals a tight labor market that increases wage costs faster than productivity, compressing corporate profit margins and generating negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% in Oct 2013 is below the 5.5% threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE is below 5.5%, or the mean return in triggered periods exceeds the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) confirms income-backed demand expansion rather than temporary consumption shifts, driving positive 20-day S&P 500 returns through upward earnings revisions.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 378148.0 and PAYEMS at 137818.0 in Oct 2013 indicate concurrent strength
failure_condition: The joint month-over-month growth signal in RSAFS and PAYEMS produces a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term does not significantly outperform the linear combination of the two series (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive monetary regime where the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS tighten financial conditions and raise equity discount rates, producing negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.28% in Oct 2013 places it in the restrictive regime
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, months with rising FEDFUNDS produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the t-statistic of the return difference between rising-rate and flat-rate months is not negative with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization that translates into higher operating leverage and corporate earnings, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO growth fails to predict the sign of 60-day forward returns with an accuracy greater than 54% over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean return following positive INDPRO growth is not statistically greater than following negative growth (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a durable construction-and-income demand loop that supports cyclical earnings, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns; either series alone is insufficient because housing can reflect interest-rate sensitivity without output validation.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 947.0 and GDPC1 at 11693.1 in 2013
failure_condition: The interaction term between quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth and month-over-month HOUST growth exhibits a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) falls below its trailing 12-month median, operating deleverage and negative earnings revisions dominate, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO drops below its trailing 12-month median
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following INDPRO below its trailing 12-month median are positive in more than 50% of observations, or the mean return in triggered months is not statistically lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) signal a stagflationary tightening of real financial conditions that compresses equity multiples and earnings expectations, generating negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 213.743 and FEDFUNDS at 2.28% in Oct 2013
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL growth produces positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).
